origin.name: North Macedonia
origin.key_document: Izvod od maticna kniga na umreni (death certificate, requires certified English translation)
origin.source: FCDO North Macedonia travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Skopje guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in North Macedonia is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is an option. You will need the death certificate, cremation certificate, and export documentation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Izvod od maticna kniga na umreni (death certificate, certified English translation required)
special_rules: North Macedonia is a Hague Apostille Convention member, which simplifies document legalisation.
Death must be registered with the Maticna sluzba (Civil Registry) promptly.
All Macedonian-language documentation requires a certified English translation for UK acceptance.
The Prosecutor's Office orders post-mortem for suspicious or violent deaths.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Skopje can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: North Macedonia to UK Repatriation: Family Guide
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in North Macedonia. Repatriation to the UK takes 1-2 weeks. Civil Registry, apostille, and documentation all handled. Contact us 24/7.
